title: Introduction to Quantum Computing
description: Introduction to quantum computing covering qubits, quantum gates, quantum circuits, and quantum algorithms. Students study the mathematical foundations of quantum mechanics relevant to computation, including linear algebra over complex vector spaces, tensor products, and measurement. Key algorithms covered include Deutsch-Jozsa, Grover's search, Shor's factoring algorithm, and quantum error correction. Both CS202 (for formal mathematical reasoning) and MATH220 (linear algebra for quantum state representations) are prerequisites.
outcomes: Represent quantum states using Dirac notation
Design quantum circuits using standard gates
Compare quantum and classical computational models
